failures.test_name: Validate ID Verification Underage Improvements - Underage Registration
failures.key_investigative_step: Computing the exact idvBirthdate from the AdminPASApiSteps.java formula and comparing against account_created (today - 10 days) — revealing the exact 18-year leap-year boundary condition
failures.notes: Root cause: idvBirthdate = today.minusYears(19).plusDays(356) lands on 2008-03-01 when today=2026-03-11, exactly 18 years before account_created (2026-03-01). Strict < 18 returns false. Fix: anchor idvBirthdate to accountCreated.minusYears(18).plusDays(1). Also: face-comparison pattern false-positively matched on generic 'Expected: a string containing' signature — consider requiring score >= 5 or keyword co-occurrence.

failures.test_name: Validation Not Met - Username criteria for Profile Auto-Moderation - Attractive Member (Auto-Approved after First Name Approval)
failures.key_investigative_step: Cross-referencing conditional rendering in approvals.blade.php with dispatchSync call in UpdateProfileEventRequest.php — realising requestData is only passed in async queue path, not sync
failures.notes: Root cause: ProcessDragynTextAssessment::dispatchSync omits Dragyn::getRequestData() — qa_reason never reaches the job. Real Dragyn called instead of mock, returns MODERATE with empty reason. approvals.blade.php @if(!empty(reason)) gates the span. Fix: pass requestData in dispatchSync path in UpdateProfileEventRequest.php

failures.test_name: Validate Email Change Restriction for New Accounts and View in Admin
failures.key_investigative_step: Pattern match at Stage 0 — exact Known Instance 1 hit. No additional investigation required. Sprint trigger b30c74a029 already validated in prior run.
failures.notes: Second consecutive failure across two branches (release/ercole → feature/release-ercole-run1fixes). Fix not yet applied. Repaired malformed JSON structure in history.json (extra closing brace orphaned this test's entry).

failures.test_name: Validate Email Change Restriction for New Accounts and View in Admin
failures.key_investigative_step: QA team identified that goToLoginPageWithUrl() reorder in b30c74a029 (SATHREE-41718) moved deleteAllCookies() to run after adminLoginPage.open() — deleting cookies on admin domain instead of member domain, so pcr_hide_after_login survived and blocked isAfterLoginPcrEligible()
failures.notes: System initially classified as FE Code (ModalManager migration 227a0ca07c) and wasted significant manual investigation time. Actual cause was a QA shared helper change unrelated to the test case itself. Key lesson: always check recent QA commits to shared helpers (AdminLoginSteps, LoginSteps, ProfileWallSteps, BasePageObject) BEFORE moving to FE/BE investigation. A shared helper change can silently affect many unrelated tests. Also: cookie was deleted on the wrong domain (admin) — member domain cookie pcr_hide_after_login survived and blocked React from rendering the AfterLoginPCR modal on return to member page. Classification was partial: domain isolation mechanism was correct, but initial direction was wrong.

failures.key_investigative_step: Comparing passing vs failing Jenkins console logs — PUT body for Zeus config 504 showed {"value":true} in passing run and {"value":false} in failing run despite step being named 'as Enabled'. Immediately pointed to step definition inversion in AdminApiConfigurationDefinitions.java:30.
failures.new_pattern_trigger: A setup step named 'as Enabled' or 'as Disabled' sends the wrong boolean value — detectable only by comparing passing vs failing console logs for Zeus/config PUT bodies.
failures.new_pattern_protocol: 1. Find the Zeus config PUT calls in the failing console log. 2. Compare the value sent against a passing run for the same step. 3. If value is inverted relative to the step name, check git log on AdminApiConfigurationDefinitions.java for recent changes. 4. Confirm with git diff — boolean was flipped. Owner: QA author of that commit.
failures.notes: Sprint trigger b8957e82bb was committed Mar 6 (same day as a passing run) but the failing test was in a different feature file that ran later. Commit message 'Updated for testing' strongly suggests a temporary debug change that was never reverted. Also: system initially confused 'Email Masked' assertThat label as the failing element — took two rounds of user correction to reach actual root cause. Lesson: always read assertThat message as a label, not as the element name.

failures.test_name: Validation of Dragyn Photo Moderation with Attractive and Generous Members
failures.key_investigative_step: Serenity recordReportData captured 'Dragyn reason as -> Minor in photo' — revealing the actual Dragyn response before any code investigation. Without this, the actual reason in the UI was unknown and led to two wrong classifications.
failures.new_pattern_trigger: Test uploads a known content_type photo with no QA override, asserts a specific Dragyn reason, but Dragyn returns a different reason (ML false positive). Distinguish from QA regression by checking git history for recent removal of x-qa-override — if never present, it is intentional E2E; if recently removed, it is a QA regression.
failures.new_pattern_protocol: 1. Get actual reason from Serenity recordReportData ('Dragyn reason as'). 2. Check git log --since='14 days ago' on upload step for recent removal of qa_override / x-qa-override — if removed recently: QA Automation regression; if never present: intentional E2E. 3. If intentional: check image file git history for recent changes. 4. If no image/BE changes: Dragyn ML false positive, owner = Dragyn team.
failures.notes: Two wrong classifications before correct answer: (1) assumed reason was empty — corrected by user pointing to Serenity recorded data; (2) assumed step definition wiring bug — corrected by QA/BE confirming no override is intentional by design. Root cause: Dragyn ML returning 'Minor in photo' for group_of_people.jpg (unchanged since 2022). Pattern promoted to dragyn-false-positive.

failures.test_name: Validate Nickname field attempts on Edit Profile And PAS Queue
failures.key_investigative_step: Recognising that ProfileWallDefinitions failure post-admin-block is the same admin-session-cookie-survival mechanism as Email Change Restriction — pcr_hide_after_login=true cookie surviving from member login through the admin block, suppressing AfterLoginPCR modal on return to member domain.
failures.notes: Original investigation was misled by 227a0ca07c (ModalManager migration, SATHREE-41561) touching AfterLoginPCR.tsx in the same sprint window. The element appeared to be a rendering regression but the modal was never eligible due to the surviving cookie. Actual sprint trigger: b30c74a029 (deleteAllCookies reorder, SATHREE-41718). QA fix attempt de9f0d3497 (WaitUntil.refreshIfElementIsUnstable) was unnecessary — the issue is not timing. Pattern admin-session-cookie-survival would have caught this immediately had it existed at the time. This is a second confirmed instance of the pattern across two different tests in the same sprint.
